title: bud: create non-existent WORKDIR with USER permissions
body:
Hi there,

I just want to cross reference a docker issue which seems to  affect `buildah bud` to
See also https://github.com/docker/cli/issues/3346

```
buildah --version
buildah version 1.23.1 (image-spec 1.0.1-dev, runtime-spec 1.0.2-dev)
```

Building the following Dockerfile with `buildah bud`

````Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/library/alpine:latest
RUN adduser -D http -h /home/http
USER http
RUN id
WORKDIR /home/http/public
RUN ls -ld $PWD
RUN touch foobar
````

fails with the following output

```bash
[ruben@k8s-cluster9-controller test]$ buildah bud
STEP 1/7: FROM docker.io/library/alpine:latest
STEP 2/7: RUN adduser -D http -h /home/http
STEP 3/7: USER http
STEP 4/7: RUN id
uid=1000(http) gid=1000(http)
STEP 5/7: WORKDIR /home/http/public
STEP 6/7: RUN ls -ld $PWD
drwxr-xr-x    2 root     root          4096 Nov  9 06:44 /home/http/public
STEP 7/7: RUN touch foobar
touch: foobar: Permission denied
error building at STEP "RUN touch foobar": error while running runtime: exit status 1
ERRO[0005] exit status 1 
```

comments.body: @r10r Interested in opening a PR to fix?

@rhatdan Yes, I can take a look at it and send a PR. 
I'll wait for @flouthoc to confirm the issue.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Sorry for the high round-trip time ;) I'll look at it between the next two weeks.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@r10r Did you ever look into it?

@rhatdan Sorry no. I'll try to send a pull request by the end of the week.
Can you please give me a hint where I can find the test suite for the bud command.

I have been playing with this and have been debating with @nalind about whether or not we should do it. The issue is whether or not Docker compatibility versus docker buildkit compatibility.
Second thing to understand it this is more complicated then I first thought and  I have been working on a potential fix.

Ok, this is good to know. I'll wait then. Let me know if I can help.

> I have been playing with this and have been debating with @nalind about whether or not we should do it

I  looked at the cross linked issue again to just realise that this is really a bug in *old* Docker engine, which
won't get fixed because they are pushing buildkit forward. Keeping full *old* Docker compatibility will always mean keeping these bugs. Well, I'm interested in outcome of your debate.
